title: exacum racemosum
intro: Exacum racemosum, also known as the white deadrippy or exacum, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Exacum racemosum include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, Exacum racemosum is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
